title: Soft Voice Hold Pause
setup: Hold baby comfortably and support head and neck.
steps: Speak softly, pause, watch baby's face and body, adjust your voice or hold.
what_to_watch: Baby calms, looks, softens body, or changes breathing pattern.
why_it_works: Warm responsive caregiving supports safety and regulation during early routines.
claim_limit: Supports regulation and caregiver responsiveness, does not treat distress.
stop_rules: Stop if baby seems uncomfortable, hungry, hot, cold, or hard to soothe.
low_energy_option: Try one soft phrase before feeding or sleep routine.

title: Face-to-Face Pause
setup: Place baby close enough to see your face during an awake calm moment.
steps: Look at baby, smile gently, pause, respond to any look or movement.
what_to_watch: Baby looks toward face, blinks, moves, or briefly smiles.
why_it_works: Face-focused interaction supports early social attention and caregiver responsiveness.
claim_limit: Supports social attention, does not measure bonding quality.
stop_rules: Stop if baby turns away, fusses, or seems overstimulated.
low_energy_option: Do one smile and one pause.

title: Copy Baby's Coos
setup: Hold baby where they can hear and see you.
steps: Wait for a sound, copy it softly, pause, respond again.
what_to_watch: Baby makes sounds, pauses, looks, or moves.
why_it_works: Back-and-forth sound play supports early communication turn-taking.
claim_limit: Supports communication prerequisites, does not teach speech on its own.
stop_rules: Stop if baby cries, coughs, turns away, or seems tired.
adaptations: Use with any safe caregiving position.

title: Sound Noticing Routine
setup: Use a gentle normal sound nearby, not loud or startling on purpose.
steps: Make or notice a sound, say what it is, watch baby's response.
what_to_watch: Baby startles, stills, blinks, changes movement, or turns later in development.
why_it_works: Naming everyday sounds supports auditory attention and caregiver observation.
claim_limit: Supports observation, does not test hearing.
stop_rules: Do not create loud sounds near baby, discuss lack of response with provider.
low_energy_option: Notice one natural sound during the day.

title: Slow Face Tracking
setup: Hold baby calm and awake on back or in arms.
steps: Move your face slowly from center to one side and back.
what_to_watch: Baby watches briefly or follows part of movement.
why_it_works: Slow visual social routines support attention and caregiver observation.
claim_limit: Supports observation, does not assess vision.
stop_rules: Stop if baby fusses or looks away repeatedly.
low_energy_option: Move your face once and smile.
adaptations: Use high-contrast toy if face is not engaging.

title: Mini Tummy Moment
setup: Place baby awake and supervised on tummy on a firm safe surface.
steps: Get low, talk softly, show your face or a toy, stop before baby is upset.
what_to_watch: Baby lifts head briefly, turns head, moves arms or legs.
why_it_works: Brief supervised prone play supports early head and trunk control.
claim_limit: Supports motor practice, does not force motor development.
stop_rules: Only awake and supervised, stop if distressed or sleepy, never use for sleep.
low_energy_option: One short tummy moment after diapering.
adaptations: Use chest-to-chest tummy time if floor is hard.

title: Smile-Copy Game
setup: Sit with baby facing you during calm awake time.
steps: Smile, wait, copy baby's expression or movement, pause.
what_to_watch: Baby smiles, moves, vocalizes, or looks to continue.
why_it_works: Responsive imitation supports social attention and turn-taking.
claim_limit: Supports interaction, does not assess attachment.
stop_rules: Stop if baby looks away, arches, cries, or seems overstimulated.
adaptations: Can be done with another caregiver.

title: Coo Conversation
setup: Hold baby where your face is visible.
steps: Make a soft vowel sound, wait, copy baby's sound, pause again.
what_to_watch: Baby coos, moves mouth, smiles, or listens.
why_it_works: Sound exchange supports early vocal turn-taking and communication attention.
claim_limit: Supports vocal practice, does not guarantee speech outcomes.
stop_rules: Stop if baby is tired, hungry, or upset.

title: Voice From Side
setup: When baby is awake, speak gently from one side at a normal volume.
steps: Say baby's name or a short phrase, wait, move to other side later.
what_to_watch: Baby stills, looks, turns head, or changes expression.
why_it_works: Everyday voice routines support auditory attention and caregiver observation.
claim_limit: Supports observation, does not replace hearing screening.
stop_rules: Do not use loud sounds, discuss lack of sound response with provider.
low_energy_option: Say one phrase from the side.

title: Hand Watching Game
setup: Place baby on back or supported in arms.
steps: Bring baby's hands into view gently, name them, pause for looking.
what_to_watch: Baby looks at hands, opens fingers, brings hands toward mouth.
why_it_works: Hand watching supports body awareness and early exploration.
claim_limit: Supports awareness, does not assess fine motor ability.
stop_rules: Do not force hands open, stop if baby resists.
adaptations: Use a soft wrist rattle only if safe and supervised.

title: Safe Mouth Explore
setup: Offer one clean baby-safe object too large to choke.
steps: Let baby look, touch, mouth safely, name texture and object.
what_to_watch: Baby brings object or hands to mouth, explores, or turns away.
why_it_works: Mouthing safe objects supports sensory exploration and hand-mouth coordination.
claim_limit: Supports exploration, does not require baby to mouth objects.
stop_rules: Use only age-safe objects, supervise, stop if gagging or distress occurs.
adaptations: Skip if baby is tired or unwell.

title: Forearm Tummy Play
setup: Place baby awake and supervised on tummy.
steps: Put toy or your face in front, encourage looking, keep session brief.
what_to_watch: Baby lifts head, pushes on forearms, turns head, or watches.
why_it_works: Prone play supports head, shoulder and trunk control.
claim_limit: Supports motor practice, does not force rolling or sitting.
stop_rules: Awake supervised only, stop if distressed, never for sleep.

title: Familiar Face Greeting
setup: Have a familiar caregiver enter calmly during awake time.
steps: Say hello, pause, let baby look and respond, name the person.
what_to_watch: Baby looks, smiles, reaches, settles, or changes expression.
why_it_works: Predictable greetings support recognition, social attention and responsive routines.
claim_limit: Supports observation of recognition, not a formal assessment.
stop_rules: Stop if baby becomes overwhelmed.
adaptations: Works with video call only as social routine, not screen time habit.

title: Mirror Hello
setup: Sit with baby supported and facing a baby-safe mirror.
steps: Say hello to reflection, smile, pause, name what baby does.
what_to_watch: Baby looks, smiles, touches mirror, vocalizes, or turns away.
why_it_works: Face-focused routines support social attention and responsive caregiving.
claim_limit: Supports interaction, does not measure self-recognition.
stop_rules: Use baby-safe mirror, stop if overstimulated.

title: Sound Turn-Taking
setup: Hold baby where they can see your face.
steps: Wait for sound or expression, copy it, pause, respond again.
what_to_watch: Baby vocalizes, smiles, kicks, looks, or pauses.
why_it_works: Back-and-forth interaction supports early communication and social learning.
claim_limit: Supports communication prerequisites, does not treat language delay.
stop_rules: Stop if baby turns away, cries, stiffens, coughs, or seems tired.

title: Side Reach Toy
setup: Place baby on back, side-lying, tummy, or supported sitting.
steps: Hold toy slightly to one side, wait for looking or reaching, celebrate attempt.
what_to_watch: Baby looks, reaches, shifts weight, or grasps.
why_it_works: Reaching supports visual attention, arm control, body awareness and problem-solving.
claim_limit: Supports reaching practice, does not require grasping every time.
stop_rules: Avoid small items, stop if tired or frustrated.

title: Responsive Feeding Pause
setup: Use normal feeding routine and watch baby cues.
steps: Offer, pause when baby slows or turns away, name the cue, continue only if baby re-engages.
what_to_watch: Baby closes mouth, turns away, reaches, opens mouth, or relaxes.
why_it_works: Responsive caregiving helps baby's cues become meaningful in interaction.
claim_limit: Supports cue reading, does not solve feeding problems.
stop_rules: If feeding is unsafe, painful, stressful, or growth is a concern, speak with provider.
adaptations: Use with milk or solids as appropriate.

title: Straight-Arm Tummy Reach
setup: Place baby awake and supervised on tummy.
steps: Put toy in front and slightly to side, encourage looking and reaching, keep brief.
what_to_watch: Baby pushes up, shifts weight, reaches, or watches.
why_it_works: Brief prone play supports shoulder, trunk and head control.
claim_limit: Supports motor practice, does not force milestone achievement.
stop_rules: Awake supervised only, stop if distressed, hungry, tired, or uncomfortable.
adaptations: Use caregiver face instead of toy.

title: Tripod Sit Practice
setup: Place baby on floor with caregiver close and support as needed.
steps: Let baby lean on hands briefly, place toy in front, end before fatigue.
what_to_watch: Baby leans on hands, lifts head, or shifts weight.
why_it_works: Supported sitting practice can support trunk control when baby shows readiness.
claim_limit: Supports readiness, does not force independent sitting.
stop_rules: Stay close, do not leave baby sitting alone, stop if slumping or distress.
adaptations: Use caregiver lap instead of floor.

title: Peekaboo Pause
setup: Sit facing baby during calm awake time.
steps: Hide face briefly, reappear, smile, wait for baby's response.
what_to_watch: Baby smiles, laughs, looks, reaches, or anticipates return.
why_it_works: Peekaboo supports social anticipation, object permanence and turn-taking.
claim_limit: Supports social play, not a test of cognition.
stop_rules: Stop if baby is startled or upset.
notes: Map note: supports CDC peekaboo milestone.

title: Name Pause Game
setup: During normal play, call baby's name warmly once.
steps: Wait without repeating too quickly, respond to any look or movement.
what_to_watch: Baby looks, stills, smiles, vocalizes, or turns.
why_it_works: Name routines support social attention and caregiver observation.
claim_limit: Supports observation, does not assess hearing or autism.
stop_rules: If baby never responds to name or sounds, discuss with provider.

title: Babble Copy
setup: Sit face-to-face or beside baby.
steps: Copy baby's sound string, add a simple word, pause for response.
what_to_watch: Baby repeats sounds, smiles, changes rhythm, or listens.
why_it_works: Imitative turn-taking supports early communication and sound play.
claim_limit: Supports communication, does not teach words directly.
stop_rules: Stop if baby is tired or frustrated.

title: Up Gesture Routine
setup: Before picking baby up, pause and show open arms.
steps: Say up, wait, respond to any reach or arm lift.
what_to_watch: Baby lifts arms, leans, vocalizes, or looks.
why_it_works: Consistent gesture routines support early communication before words.
claim_limit: Supports gesture communication, does not replace speech evaluation.
stop_rules: Stop if baby is upset or unsafe to wait.

title: Drop-and-Find Game
setup: Sit with baby safely supported.
steps: Drop toy within sight, pause, ask where it went, help baby look.
what_to_watch: Baby looks down, searches, reaches, or anticipates.
why_it_works: Searching for dropped objects supports attention and object permanence.
claim_limit: Supports exploration, not a cognition test.
stop_rules: Use safe object, avoid throwing near face, stop if frustrated.
adaptations: Use high chair only if safely seated.

title: Pass-the-Toy
setup: Offer toy at midline while baby sits supported or on floor.
steps: Let baby take it, offer from other side, wait for transfer.
what_to_watch: Baby grasps, transfers, bangs, or explores.
why_it_works: Object transfer supports bilateral coordination and exploration.
claim_limit: Supports practice, does not assess hand function alone.
stop_rules: Stop if baby is tired, avoid small parts.
low_energy_option: One pass from hand to hand.

title: Sit-and-Reach Play
setup: Place baby sitting on floor with close supervision.
steps: Put toys slightly in front and to side, let baby reach and return.
what_to_watch: Baby sits, reaches, adjusts balance, or uses hands.
why_it_works: Sitting with reach supports trunk control, balance and purposeful exploration.
claim_limit: Supports balance practice, does not force sitting.
stop_rules: Stay close, avoid hard surfaces, stop if baby slumps or falls repeatedly.

title: Pat-a-Cake Pause
setup: Sit facing baby in a calm playful moment.
steps: Clap slowly, pause, wait for baby to join, copy any attempt.
what_to_watch: Baby claps, smiles, watches, vocalizes, or anticipates.
why_it_works: Simple social games support turn-taking, imitation and shared attention.
claim_limit: Supports social play, does not require perfect clapping.
stop_rules: Stop if baby loses interest or is frustrated.

title: Bye-Bye Routine
setup: Use a real departure or object disappearing routine.
steps: Wave slowly, say bye-bye, pause for any gesture or look.
what_to_watch: Baby waves, looks, vocalizes, smiles, or watches.
why_it_works: Repeated meaningful gestures support communication in everyday routines.
claim_limit: Supports gesture use, does not replace language evaluation.
stop_rules: Do not drill, stop if baby is upset by leaving.

title: Name-and-Respond
setup: During play, label caregiver names naturally.
steps: Say mama, dada, or chosen caregiver name, wait, respond to baby's sound.
what_to_watch: Baby vocalizes, approximates, points, looks, or smiles.
why_it_works: Responsive naming supports word meaning through warm repeated routines.
claim_limit: Supports language routines, does not guarantee words.
stop_rules: Stop if baby is frustrated, avoid pressure to perform.

title: In-and-Out Container Play
setup: Place a few large safe objects and a container on floor.
steps: Show one object going in, pause, let baby try, dump out together.
what_to_watch: Baby puts in, takes out, bangs, looks, or imitates.
why_it_works: Container play supports problem-solving, hand use and cause-effect learning.
claim_limit: Supports exploration, not task performance.
stop_rules: Use non-choking objects, supervise, stop if frustrated.
adaptations: Use laundry basket and soft toys.

title: Hide-and-Find Toy
setup: Let baby see you partly hide a toy under cloth.
steps: Ask where it is, pause, let baby uncover or help.
what_to_watch: Baby looks, reaches, lifts cloth, or smiles.
why_it_works: Hide-and-find supports object permanence and shared attention.
claim_limit: Supports problem-solving, not a cognition test.
stop_rules: Stop if baby is upset, avoid covering face.

title: Stable Pull-to-Stand Play
setup: Use low stable furniture and stay close.
steps: Place toy on safe surface, let baby pull or kneel if ready, support as needed.
what_to_watch: Baby kneels, pulls, stands with support, or cruises.
why_it_works: Supported standing opportunities can support strength and balance when baby shows readiness.
claim_limit: Supports readiness, does not push early walking.
stop_rules: Use only stable furniture, stay close, stop if baby is tired or unsafe.

title: Pincer Snack Practice
setup: Seat baby safely for eating with caregiver present.
steps: Offer one safe small soft food piece, wait, let baby attempt pickup.
what_to_watch: Baby rakes, pinches, touches, or brings food to mouth.
why_it_works: Self-feeding practice supports fine motor control and responsive feeding.
claim_limit: Supports fine motor practice, not feeding therapy.
stop_rules: Use safe foods, supervise closely, follow choking guidance, stop if unsafe.
adaptations: Use non-food large object if feeding is not appropriate.
